item.message: Skill descriptions were shortened to fit the 2% skills context budget. Codex can still see every skill, but some descriptions are shorter. Disable unused skills or plugins to leave more room for the rest.

item.text: I’ll inspect the three requested source files, make the minimal schema/model/query changes, then run the test suite.

item.command: /bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,240p' src/schema.mjs && sed -n '1,240p' src/accounts.mjs && sed -n '1,260p' src/queries.mjs"
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,240p' src/schema.mjs && sed -n '1,240p' src/accounts.mjs && sed -n '1,260p' src/queries.mjs"

item.text: The account mapping, writes, and summary projection now include the optional field; the database migration adds a nullable checked column. I’m running the benchmark tests.

item.command: /bin/zsh -lc 'npm test'
/bin/zsh -lc 'npm test'

item.command: /bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' tests/acceptance.test.mjs && git diff -- src/schema.mjs src/accounts.mjs src/queries.mjs"
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' tests/acceptance.test.mjs && git diff -- src/schema.mjs src/accounts.mjs src/queries.mjs"

item.text: The first test exposed a pre-existing schema mismatch: `accounts.created_at` is required but has no default, while the established account API does not supply it. I’ll preserve the API by setting it explicitly during account creation, then rerun the suite.

item.command: /bin/zsh -lc 'npm test'

item.text: The regression fixture also relies on the existing `orders.currency` default. I restored that schema default so its legacy insert remains valid, and am checking the full flow again.
